trials.supervisor_analysis: Baseline established at score 60.0. The agent's manual Triton kernel was 137x slower than PyTorch. With only ~32 minutes remaining, advancing to optimization stage with specific guidance rather than spending more time on profiling.

nudges.text: Your manual Triton kernel is 137x slower than PyTorch's optimized depthwise conv. Before spending more time on manual kernel tuning, try `torch.compile` as your first optimization lever — it generates Triton kernels under the hood and often matches or beats hand-written kernels for standard ops. You can wrap the forward method with `torch.compile(mode="default")` in `ModelNew` and benchmark that first to establish what's achievable, then decide if manual kernel work is worthwhile.

The GPU memory fault may be from leftover corrupted state from earlier bad kernel launches. Try a simple clean approach: make `ModelNew.forward` just call `self.conv2d(x)` (using `nn.Conv2d` with `groups=in_channels`) and wrap it with `torch.compile(mode="default")`. No custom Triton kernel needed — `torch.compile` will generate one. Also consider adding `torch.cuda.synchronize()` or restarting the process to clear GPU state before testing.
